tasks.title: Add pointer event handlers to PullToRefresh.svelte
tasks.description: Add handlePointerDown, handlePointerMove, handlePointerUp, and handleLostPointerCapture handler functions to PullToRefresh.svelte. All four handlers must guard on pointerType === 'mouse' to prevent double-fire on hybrid/touch devices. handlePointerDown calls setPointerCapture(event.pointerId) and enters pending gesture mode (reuses existing startTouchY, pendingGesture, isTracking, pullDistance, gestureEmoji state variables). handlePointerMove resolves pending gestures and accumulates damped pull distance (same logic as handleTouchMove, reusing applyPullDamping and pickRandomPullEmoji). handlePointerUp settles the gesture via doRefresh() or retract (same logic as handleTouchEnd). handleLostPointerCapture cancels cleanly (same logic as handleTouchCancel). Register all four listeners in onMount with correct passive/non-passive options (pointermove must be non-passive for preventDefault). Clean up all four listeners in the onMount destructor.
tasks.done_when: Four pointer event handlers are registered in onMount and cleaned up on destroy. All handlers filter on pointerType === 'mouse'. setPointerCapture is called on pointerdown. The gesture lifecycle (pending -> tracking -> settle/cancel) works identically to the touch path. Existing touch handlers are unchanged. Component compiles without type errors (npm run check passes).

tasks.title: Add CSS cursor feedback and user-select suppression
tasks.description: Add an isPointerDragging boolean state variable to PullToRefresh.svelte. Set it to true when pointer tracking activates (in handlePointerMove when pendingGesture resolves to tracking), and false when the gesture ends (in handlePointerUp and handleLostPointerCapture). Apply the class ptr-dragging to .ptr-wrap via class:ptr-dragging={isPointerDragging}. Add scoped CSS rules inside @media (pointer: fine): .ptr-wrap gets cursor: grab; .ptr-wrap.ptr-dragging gets cursor: grabbing and user-select: none.
tasks.done_when: The ptr-wrap element shows cursor:grab on desktop (fine pointer) at rest, and cursor:grabbing + user-select:none during active pointer drag. The class is toggled correctly by the isPointerDragging variable. No cursor changes on touch devices. Component compiles without type errors.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for pointer gesture lifecycle
tasks.description: Add a new describe('PullToRefresh pointer gesture', ...) block in PullToRefresh.test.ts. Tests should cover: (1) pointer gesture start/move/end triggers refresh when pull exceeds threshold, (2) pointer gesture retracts when pull is below threshold, (3) pointerType filtering -- events with pointerType 'touch' or 'pen' are ignored, (4) setPointerCapture is called on pointerdown, (5) lostpointercapture cancels the gesture without refresh, (6) pointer gesture does not activate when scrollTop > 0, (7) isRefreshing blocks new pointer gestures. Use fireEvent or manual dispatchEvent with PointerEvent constructor (setting pointerType in the constructor options).
tasks.done_when: All new pointer gesture tests pass (npm run test). Tests cover the seven scenarios listed. Existing touch tests continue to pass unchanged.

tasks.title: Add E2E test for desktop PTR mouse gesture
tasks.description: Add a new test in web/tests/e2e/pull-to-refresh.spec.ts (or a new test block within it) that uses Playwright's page.mouse API to simulate a desktop click-and-drag PTR gesture. The test should: use a desktop viewport (not the Pixel 5 device profile used by touch tests), navigate to '/', verify the PTR indicator starts hidden, simulate a mouse down + drag down past the threshold + mouse up on .ptr-wrap, and verify that a refresh is triggered (indicator appears, then settles). Tag with @smoke. Existing touch PTR test must remain unchanged and continue to pass.
tasks.done_when: New E2E test passes in Chromium (npm run test:e2e:smoke). Existing touch PTR smoke test continues to pass. The test uses page.mouse (not touch) and a desktop viewport.

history.note: Feature initiated: Enable pull-to-refresh on desktop viewports. Currently PTR only handles touch events, so mouse/trackpad users on desktop have no way to trigger the gesture.
Advanced to discovery. Routing to product-manager for requirements gathering and exec plan creation.
Advanced to design. Routing to principal-engineer for technical design.
Advanced to tasks. Broke design into 4 tasks: T1 pointer handlers, T2 CSS cursor/user-select, T3 unit tests, T4 E2E test.
Starting T1: Add pointer event handlers to PullToRefresh.svelte
